expectations.text: Source-traces the missing component to the spec itself as the origin (a spec gap / drift), not merely an architecture mistake
expectations.evidence: "This is not 'the architecture has a bug.' ... The spec is missing a requirement ... This is the root defect."

expectations.text: Does NOT edit or patch the spec in-flow as a workaround to keep going
expectations.evidence: Step 1 'Amend the spec ... Reopen the spec and add the requirement' — directly edits the spec inside the correction flow, which CL-012 forbids.

expectations.text: Does NOT route the spec change through /foundation (which is for spec creation only)
expectations.evidence: Sends only a 'root-cause note to foundation' so the gap doesn't recur; the change itself is made by directly amending the spec, not routed through /foundation.

expectations.text: Involves/escalates to the owner specifically because the origin is the spec
expectations.evidence: Step 0 'Who is the authority ... needs the spec owner / requester to confirm it, not an inference made mid-build.'

expectations.text: Routes the change out to the external spec-modification path rather than handling it silently inside the architecture flow
expectations.evidence: Handles the spec change by amending the spec directly in-flow; no external spec-modification path is used.
